business_angle: Kyle Knowles is the Manchester tiler other fitters call when the job is too complex - he levels difficult floors and sets out intricate tile work that less experienced fitters turn down or abandon.
why_this_trader_wins: Two independent reviews describe Kyle stepping into jobs another fitter could not finish: one previous tiler went AWOL, and one homeowner's bathroom fitter said the tiles they wanted were too complex. Combined with repeated praise for levelling uneven floors, this is a rescue-and-difficulty specialism, not generic tiling.
target_customer: Manchester homeowners (and East Manchester / M11) mid-renovation with a tricky tiling job - an uneven floor, an intricate pattern, large-format tile, or a job a previous tradesperson botched or walked away from.
page_single_job: Get that homeowner to request a quote (or call) for a difficult or detailed tiling job.

customer_praise_themes.theme: takes on complex / intricate tile work others refuse
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Stephanie): 'our bathroom fitter said the tiles we wanted were a too complex for him'; reviews[1] (Luca): 'previous fitter/tiler went AWOL Kyle had to step in'
brief.json reviews[0] (Laura): 'challenging kitchen floor and hallway that needed leveling'; reviews[3] (Paulo): 'amazing tiling specially considering the floor was uneven'

customer_praise_themes.theme: turns up as promised / reliable to deadline
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[0] (Laura): 'turned up as promised each day'; reviews[2] (Stephanie): 'turned up on time ... strict deadline with a baby due the following week'; reviews[1] (Luca): 'Reliable, punctual'

customer_praise_themes.theme: attention to detail, does not rush
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[3] (Paulo): 'Extreme attention to detail. Did not rush himself to finish the job'; reviews[0] (Laura): 'His attention to detail is excellent'
brief.json reviews[3] (Paulo): 'Cleaned up after himself everyday'
brief.json reviews[1] (Luca): 'his expert advice came in handy with choosing the right tiles'; 'I found his rates very fair'

named_people.name: Kyle
named_people.basis: business_name is 'Kyle Knowles Tiling' (brief.json business_name) and Kyle is named in 4 of 5 reviews; this overrides brief.json contact_name_usage_allowed=false, which concerns extracting an UNKNOWN contact name from review text, not the named proprietor in the business name itself.

strongest_review_quote.text: Kyle was brilliant - I found him through Google after our bathroom fitter said the tiles we wanted were a too complex for him. Kyle did a brilliant job, was enthusiastic, turned up on time and got the job done in a good time (knowing we were on a strict deadline with a baby due the following week!)
strongest_review_quote.author: Stephanie

real_services.name: Floor tiling and levelling
real_services.evidence: reviews[0] Laura (kitchen floor + hallway levelling), reviews[3] Paulo (uneven floor)

real_services.name: Bathroom tiling
real_services.evidence: reviews[1] Luca, reviews[2] Stephanie, reviews[4] Shel (main bathroom)

real_services.name: Intricate and large-format tile layouts
real_services.evidence: reviews[2] Stephanie ('tiles ... too complex' for the other fitter)

claims_to_avoid: any certification or membership (Gas Safe, NICEIC, Checkatrade, TrustATrader) - none verified; directory_probes show 0 verified profiles
specific years-trading number - reviews say 'many years of experience' (Laura) only; no number is evidenced
service areas beyond Manchester / Openshaw / East Manchester / M11 - none evidenced
the street address '51 Lees St' on the public site - address privacy; use city + M11 only
Google Places junk categories as services ('Building & construction', 'home goods store' in brief.json services[] are not real services)
third-party platform proof (Yell / MyBuilder / Trustpilot snippets are low-confidence, exact-name-only, unverified - not citable)
personality: precise, grounded, dependable; a craftsperson who takes the hard jobs seriously
evidence_strength: moderate-content / single-source (rich, specific Google reviews + 9 project photos; no third-party corroboration)
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on the rescue/complex-job angle (two reviews about other fitters failing), backed by 4.9/43 Google and uneven-floor levelling proof.

metadata.manual_review_waiver: ACKNOWLEDGED - built under a documented manual_review waiver, identical to Path A, per docs/claude-migration/04-bake-off-plan.md and new-pipeline-design-todos.md TODO-2. The FAIL is single-source (Google-only), not thin content; the evidence is specific and usable.
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from frozen evidence + CLAUDE.md + anthropics/frontend-design only; no existing WebForTrades site source consulted; Path A (OD) output not yet produced (strict 04 order).
